mentions.note: Slammed over AI displacement fears; market irrationally treats AI as existential threat to software companies.
Enterprise software weakness; stock not doing well on the charts.
Enterprise software suffering extreme P/E multiple shrinkage on irrational AI disruption fears despite no earnings misses; momentum money fleeing to storage; psychological, not fundamental.
Won $5.6B 10-year Army contract for AI-powered procurement software; out-of-nowhere win shows Pentagon turning to commercial software solutions; fell 87 cents despite win due to SaaS sector weakness.
Same multiple compression story as ServiceNow; great company facing market skepticism on per-user SaaS model sustainability; wait for multiple stabilization.

#7 worst January, down ~20%. P/E fell 30x → 16x forward. Enterprise software under siege from AI displacement fears. Cramer's travel trust owns it for years but multiple compression severe; could argue for overshooting downside at these levels.
Down 7% as P/E multiples compress on AI displacement fears; no earnings cuts yet but P/E floor unknown; declines may be overdone but timing uncertain.
Down 43%, 14x P/E (lowest in history including 2008); 12% earnings growth (slight below S&P); own for Travel Trust; near bottom.
Own for Travel Trust; down 43%, 14x earnings (lowest multiple in history including 2008); 12% growth slightly slower than S&P; near bottom; good entry point.
Lost 33% YTD, now trades under 15x earnings (cheap for growth stock), but earnings may reflect SaaS per-user pressure from AI efficiency gains; watch Wednesday earnings carefully.
Enterprise software under threat from Anthropic AI; clients shortening contracts; stock face structural headwinds from AI-driven code generation and automation capabilities.

Agent Force generating $800M ARR, up 169% YoY with 29K deals since Sept 2024. Clients (Wyndham Hotels, Shark Ninja) proving AI agents handle drudgery, freeing humans for high-value work. $50B buyback signals CEO conviction. SaaS apocalypse narrative overblown; Salesforce differentiated by domain-specific AI + compliance expertise. Company has track record of bouncing back; mistake to count them out.

Dropped 10 points on earnings (191 to 182) despite excellent quarter and $50B buyback. Rallied +7.82 to close at $199. Program buying drove recovery after program selling spike. Stock rebounded on fundamentals + buyback credibility.
Rebounded from $181 to $196 after 'not great' quarter; not-as-feared quarter results fuel recovery.
Enterprise software bounce; not going out of business despite AI competition fears; valuation reset justified.
CEO Bill McDermott executing well; company represents actual value at current prices; not a clear long-term hold but viable position.
Mentioned as part of enterprise software 'charnel house of pain'; traditional CRM model threatened by AI agents and natural-language access; still profitable but equity story deteriorating.
Record quarter, $46B revenue guidance, $16B cash flow; Agent Force driving agentic workforce adoption (29K+ users); $50B buyback signals undervaluation.

Down 4% despite strong AI capabilities (Einstein AI, Slack integration). SaaS sector guilty-until-proven-innocent on AI disruption fears; Cramer sees best Einstein capabilities but model concerns linger.
Software sector headwinds; down 3%; however Aru's research suggests enterprise buyers have 2x more confidence in Salesforce vs. new AI vendors—may be deeper value than consensus.
Nico's portfolio: unclear if Mark Benioff can deploy data center; software under pressure; Cramer not convinced; will be hard own.
Nico's position; uncertain on CEO Marc Benioff's ability to build data center business; software sector under pressure; hard own going forward.
$50B buyback (1/3 accelerated) should support stock; up $7.86 today but down 34% YTD; selective divisions disruptable by AI.
Caller Towanda has Tableau RSU position near cost basis. Cramer holding for long-term (2030+ timeframe per Marc Benioff's AI vision). Stock bottoming at cheap valuations; don't sell.
Market hates software across the board (Palantir, ServiceNow, Salesforce, Workday, Adobe). Cramer notes it's one of his smallest positions; won't push software plays.

Marc Benioff's credible leadership during Great Recession correctly predicted cloud software would be industry future; demonstrates value of listening to honest, reliable executives with track records.
Part of software rotation; up 3.5% today. Rally felt like giant short squeeze rather than fundamental endorsement.
Enterprise software vendor facing disruption from AI; executives questioning ROI of expensive per-user licensing when AI agents can replace work.
Revenue +14% YoY, $11B quarter, Agent Force >$1B revenue, record buyback; trading 12x earnings with strong guidance; Cramer owns in trust, thinks sellers are wrong.
Stunning strength as software sector recovers from recent weakness; magnificent performance after month of punishment suggests sector inflection point.

Fortress entry at 173 after short-covering bounce to 200+; hold Chapel Trust position despite 'lukewarm' guidance. $50B remaining buyback authority; agentic AI (Einstein) and Slack integration support H2 re-acceleration. Conviction beats regret.
High-quality cloud software company that became too expensive in late 2021 rally; should have been trimmed into strength; good company but better buying opportunity after pullbacks.
2021 cloud-software bull run was followed by severe drawdown post-Fed tightening; selling strength would have protected gains despite solid fundamentals.
